title: Decadent Yeasty French Lentils Tartiflette
description: A yeasty, juicy, and indulgent French dish featuring Puy lentils, toast, and a FODMAP-friendly recipe, in the form of a tartiflette.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, combine the lentils and enough water to cover them, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
In a small bowl, mix the yeast with 1 tablespoon of warm water.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat, and add the chopped onion and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cooked lentils, yeast mixture, salt, and pepper.
Transfer the lentil mixture to a baking dish, and top with the crumbly Reblochon cheese.
Arrange the bread slices on top of the cheese.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted.
